title: Mayor, Council and Auditor Thank Senate For One-House Budget Proposal
summary: Mayor Sharon Owens, Common Council President Rita Paniagua, Finance Chair Corey Williams, and City Auditor Alexander Marion publicly thanked the New York State Senate for its One-House budget proposal allocating $30 million in additional aid to Syracuse—double Governor Hochul's executive budget commitment. Senator Rachel May championed the funding, which officials stated will support infrastructure, housing, neighborhood revitalization, and essential municipal services.

political_signal.description: State Senate Democratic Majority committed $30M to Syracuse in One-House budget proposal, doubling governor's executive budget allocation. Reflects prioritization of Syracuse municipal needs and partnership between local and state leadership.

processing_notes: Dollar amount extracted as $30M (30 million); quoted as doubling governor's proposal but absolute amount of executive budget not stated in document
Author listed as Unknown; source is official City of Syracuse press release
